seoTitle: Remove Duplicate Values in Power BI Using Power Query
metaDescription: Learn how to remove duplicate values in Power BI using Power Query, keeping your datasets clean and your reports accurate.
intro: Duplicate values can quietly skew your Power BI reports, inflating totals and distorting analysis. This guide covers how to use Power Query to identify and remove duplicates, ensuring your data stays clean and your reporting stays accurate.

steps.heading: Access Power Query in Power BI
steps.body: Open Power Query Editor from within Power BI to begin working directly with your dataset's structure and content.

steps.heading: Follow the step-by-step duplicate removal process
steps.body: Select the relevant column or columns, then use Power Query's remove duplicates function to eliminate repeated entries.

steps.heading: Understand why clean reporting matters
steps.body: Removing duplicates ensures your reports reflect accurate figures, which is essential when analyzing sales, customer data, or performance metrics.

steps.heading: Explore use cases in business analysis
steps.body: Clean data is especially important when consolidating information from multiple sources, where duplicate entries are more likely to occur.

steps.heading: Automate the data cleaning process
steps.body: Once your duplicate removal steps are set up in Power Query, they apply automatically each time your data refreshes, keeping your dataset consistently clean.

faq.question: Does removing duplicates in Power Query affect the original data source?
faq.answer: No, Power Query transformations apply within Power BI and don't modify your original external data source.

faq.question: Can I remove duplicates based on multiple columns at once?
faq.answer: Yes, Power Query allows you to select multiple columns so duplicates are identified based on the combination of values across them.

faq.question: Will duplicate removal steps reapply automatically on refresh?
faq.answer: Yes, once set up in Power Query, the duplicate removal step becomes part of your query and runs automatically each time the data refreshes.

faq.question: How do I know if my data actually has duplicate values?
faq.answer: Reviewing your dataset in Power Query Editor or checking for unexpectedly inflated totals in your report are both good ways to spot duplicates.
